content: Anya presented vendor consolidation win: cut 14 SaaS subscriptions, saved $47K annually. Moved 90% of ops to Notion, Linear, Slack core. AWS spend down 12% after DevOps cleanup.
summary: Vendor consolidation complete: $47K savings, streamlined stack.

content: SOC2 audit timeline slipped 6 weeks. Root cause: Sentry logging gaps + Cloudflare config documentation was missing. Need pre-audit checklist: vendor readiness assessment → config audit → 2-week buffer.
summary: SOC2 lessons: vendor readiness + config docs critical, build buffer.

content: Realized hiring pipeline now my highest-ROI work. Priya + I closed 3 offers in 2 weeks (James, Tom, one contract eng). Async recruiting docs saved ~8 hrs of retro context-setting.
summary: Best skill: building hiring process. Docs kill context tax.

content: Still weary on compliance. SOC2 felt like 10 weeks of triage vs. strategy. But vendor consolidation felt good—clear ROI, visible process. Suspect I'm energized by optimization, drained by regulation.
summary: Energized by ops optimization; drained by compliance work.

content: Retro with Ari, Devon, James: backend infra scaled 40% this Q without hire until March. James' first week was productive—paired on Anthropic integration, no onboarding friction.
summary: Ari's team scaled well; James onboarding smooth.

content: Documented hiring playbook: Priya sources → async video screen (async, not sync!) → technical + culture (Ari) → offer + close. No meeting until offer stage. 7-day cycle possible.
Marcus asked why CS metrics aren't in dashboard. Realized I've been managing vendors but not exposing ops data to the team. That's a gap—ops should be visible.
summary: Missing: ops visibility. Metrics hidden, not shared.

content: 18 months in: I'm good at systems (vendor stack, hiring process, budget tracking). Still learning: stakeholder comfort with risk (SOC2 stress = perfectionism), and when to push back vs. absorb.
summary: Strength: systems. Gap: risk tolerance and pushback.

content: Standard retro format now: 3 questions async in Notion (what worked, what didn't, what's unclear). Sebastien + team respond. Discuss blockers only. Saves 45 mins, better notes.
summary: Async retro format: Notion 3-Q, discuss blockers only.

content: Lina flagged: Acme churn risk if onboarding stays 3 weeks. I'd accepted it; she pushed back. Reminded me that ops work for product, not the reverse. Need to sync intent more.
summary: Ops serves product. Avoid optimizing in a vacuum.

content: What got better: team trust. Ari asks me before vendor changes now. Priya's recruiting me for hiring calls. Marcus checking in on CS tools. Six months ago this was transactional.
summary: Built trust. Team sees ops as partner, not gatekeeper.

content: Still hard: saying no. SOC2 scope creep happened because I didn't push back on audit firm's 'nice-to-haves'. Cost us 3 weeks. Next vendor engagement, I'm being explicit about scope boundaries.
summary: Growth edge: push back on scope creep early.
